variant.handshake_hint: Use tls-token for maximum active-probe resistance (M8).

generation_result.reasoning: Step-by-step design rationale: transport, handshake, padding, fallback decisions...

m1_ndpi.explanation: TLS 1.3 with chrome-120 fingerprint mimics standard HTTPS traffic effectively.

m2_suricata.explanation: Non-empty SNI (www.google.com) and correct ALPN settings avoid Suricata detections.

m3_zeek.explanation: Chrome-120 fingerprint matches real browser JA3 signatures precisely.

m4_ml.explanation: Chrome-browsing padding profile creates natural packet size variation.

m6_kl_len.explanation: Mimicry padding closely matches standard HTTPS length distributions.

m8_probe.explanation: Reverse-proxy fallback serves genuine Google HTTPS responses to probes.

known_weaknesses: Chrome-browsing padding might be less effective against high-accuracy ML models trained on large datasets
TLS 1.3 is newer and may raise suspicion in legacy network monitoring tools
Active probing can still detect anomalies despite fallback, depending on probe sophistication
